notes: four. Chand Minar (2nd largest minaret in India) was built in 1435 by Ala-ud-din Bahmani to commemorate the victory over the Daulatabad Fort. It has 24 chambers, spread over four floors and a small mosque. Q.2014. Theravada is known as the official religion of which of the following neighbouring countries of India? SSC MTS 04/11/2024 (2nd Shift) (a) Afghanistan (b) Bangladesh (c) Pakistan (d) Sri Lanka Sol.2014.(d) Sri Lanka. Theravāda is one of the major branches of Buddhism, and it is considered the oldest and most traditional form. It is predominantly practiced in Southeast Asian countries such as Sri Lanka, Thailand, Myanmar (Burma), Laos, and Cambodia. Official religion of India’s Neighbouring Countries: Pakistan (Islam), Bangladesh (Islam), Bhutan (Mahayana Buddhism), Afghanistan (Islam). Q.2015. The Kamakshi Saunsthan temple of Goa is crowned with the ________ two-storied tower and with a golden Kalash perched on its Shikhar. SSC MTS 12/11/2024 (2nd Shift) (a) pentagonal (b) circular (c) hexagonal (d) octagonal Sol.2015.(d) octagonal. The Kamakshi Saunsthan temple, built in the late 16th century, is located in the village of Shiroda, Goa. The temple complex, known as 'Sthal' or 'Thal', is accessed through a grand Mahadwar (temple gate) and includes key features like Sabha Mandap (entrance hall), Deepasthambh (lamp tower), a holy water tank, and a square-shaped chowk for Prasad rituals. SSC CGL 23/09/2024 (1st Shift) (a) Only 1 and 3 (b) Only 2 and 3 (c) Only 1 (d) Only 2 Sol.2016.(b) Only and 3. Online Dispute Resolution handbook is launched by NITI Ayog. It is developed by Agami and Omidyar India, in association with NITI Aayog and with the support of ICICI Bank, Ashoka Innovators for the Public, Trilegal, Dalberg, Dvara and NIPFP.

Three-tier system. The healthcare system in India is a three-tier structure, with Sub Health Centres, Primary Health Centres, and Community Health Centres, both in urban and rural areas, serving as the pillars of the primary health care system.

Telugu. Telugu Language Day is celebrated every year on August 29. In 2008, Telugu was granted the status of a classical language. Nicolo di Conti, who visited the Vijayanagara Empire, found that the words in the Telugu language end with vowels, just like those in Italian, and hence referred to it as "The Italian of the East”.

Geographic Information System (GIS) allows for the effective mapping, analysis, and management of land data by integrating spatial and attribute information. Blockchain technology can enhance security and transparency, and VR and AR have potential applications.

Climate & environment communication. Kalpana-1 satellite was originally known as MetSat-1. Launched by vehicle PSLV - C4. Marathi is a language spoken in the Indian state of Maharashtra and belongs to the Indo-Aryan branch of the Indo-European language family. It is closely related to languages such as Hindi, Gujarati, and Punjabi. Austric: These are the languages spoken by the Munda or Kol group and spoken in central, eastern, and northeastern India. Q.2025. With which of the following communities is the word 'Langar' (community kitchen) mainly associated? SSC CHSL 05/07/2024 (3rd shift) (a) Sikhism (b) Hinduism (c) Jainism (d) Buddhism Sol.2025.(a) Sikhism. The concept of “Langar” is an integral part of Sikh culture, symbolizing equality, service, and community bonding. It is commonly served at Gurdwaras (Sikh temples) and was introduced by Guru Nanak (1469-1539). Similar tradition of Annadanam (food donation) exist in Hinduism.

1993. The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) was established on October 12, 1993. Aadhaar card is a 12-digit unique identification number issued by the Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) to every individual resident of India. It serves as proof of identity, which is linked to the individual's biometric and demographic information. The Aadhaar programme was launched in 2009 by the UIDAI with the goal of providing a unique and verifiable identity for every resident of India. Q.2032. SSC CHSL 11/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) I, II and III are correct (b) Both I and II are correct. (c) Both II and III are correct. (d) Only III is correct. Sol.2032.(a) I, II and III are correct. Radio broadcasting was changed to All India Radio (AIR) in and to Akashwani in 1957. AIR (Akashvani) is India's national public radio broadcaster. It was owned by the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting. AIR originated programming in 23 languages and 179 dialects.

Bodhidharma. He was a semi-legendary Buddhist monk who lived during the 5th or 6th century CE. In Indian philosophy, he is commonly considered the founder of Chan Buddhism in China. It was later known as Zen in Japan. Bodhidharma is also credited with founding the famous Shaolin school of Chinese martial arts. He is known as a Tripitaka Dharma Master.

Chota-Nagpur plateau. This plateau covers parts of Jharkhand, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, and West Bengal. Mundari is a Munda language, belonging to the Austroasiatic language family. Chhota Nagpur plateau is called the “Heart of industrial India” because it is rich in minerals and power fuels. The highest mountain peak of Chota Nagpur Plateau is Parasnath.

Maru-Gurjara architecture. This style of architecture evolved in the region of Rajasthan and Gujarat, blending regional traditions with elements of Hindu, Jain, and Mughal architecture. The term "Maru" refers to the desert region (Marwar) of Rajasthan, and "Gurjara" refers to the region of Gujarat, highlighting the cultural influence from both areas.

Kautilya, also known as Chanakya, outlined the Saptanga theory of the state in the Arthashastra, identifying seven key components: Swami (King), Amatya (Minister), Janapada (Territory), Durga (Fort), Kosa (Treasure), Danda (Army or Force), and Mitra (Ally). Hindu temples can be characterised under broad categories - Nagara Style (northern India), Dravida, and Vesara style (combination of both Nagara and Dravida, its superstructure is bell-shaped). Q.470. Diwan-I-Am, containing the marble canopy ornamented with beautiful panels of pietra dura work, is a part of which monument? SSC MTS 23/10/2024 (2nd Shift) (a) Bibi ka Makbara (b) Red Fort (c) Taj Mahal (d) Humayun Tomb Sol.470.(b) Red Fort. The Diwan-i-Am (Hall of Public Audience) is part of the Red Fort in Delhi. It is a significant structure that was used by the Mughal emperors for public meetings. Pietra dura is an Italian term that refers to a decorative art technique of inlaying cut, polished, colored stones into marble or other hard surfaces to create images.

Qutub Minar (Delhi). It has a diameter of 14.32 m at the base and about 2.75 m on the top with a height of 72.5 m. Qutbu'd-Din Aibak laid the foundation of Minar in AD 1199. Q.472. Which of the following statements is/are correct temples of Khajuraho? a) Equal importance is given to human experience and spiritual pursuit. History b) Jain and Hindu temples are also found at Khajuraho. c) Chausanth Yogini temple is one of the important temples here. SSC MTS 11/11/2024 (3rd Shift) (a) All a, b and c (b) Only a and b (c) Only c and a (d) Only b and c Sol.472.(a) All a, b and c. Khajuraho’s temples are also known for their extensive erotic sculptures. Khajuraho’s sculptures are highly stylised with typical features. Notable examples include the Kandariya Mahadeo temple, dedicated to Lord Shiva, and the Lakshmana temple, dedicated to Vishnu. Q.473. The title of ‘Chief of the Merchants’ or ‘Malikut-Tujjar’ was conferred on Mahmud Gawan by which of the following Bahamani kings ? SSC MTS 12/11/2024 (2nd Shift) (a) Mohammad Shah (b) Humayun Shah (c) NizamuddinAhmad III (d) Bahman Shah Sol.473.(b) Humayun Shah. Mahmud Gawan (Khawaja Mahmud Gilani), a Persian trader who arrived in Deccan, rose to prominence in the Bahmani kingdom. Under Humayun Shah's reign, he was appointed as Prime Minister with complete control over military affairs and received the title 'Malik-ut-Tujjar' (Chief of Merchants).

a-ii; b-iv; c-iii; d-i. Some other ancient books and their authors : ‘Akbar-Namah’ - Abul Fazal, ‘Buddhacharita’ - Ashvaghosha, ‘Prithviraj Raso’ - Chand Bardai, ‘Geet Govinda’ - Jaidev, ‘Rajtarangini’ - Kalhana, ‘Kumarasambhavam’ - Kalidasa, ‘Ashtadhyayi’ - Panini, and ‘Mahabhasya’ - Patanjali. Q.475. According to which of the following foreign travellers, people of Delhi used to write letters containing abuses to the Sultan, therefore, in order to punish them Sultan decided to shift the capital? SSC CGL 11/09/2024 (3rd Shift) (a) Isami (b) Hasan Nizami (c) Ibn Batuta (d) Al Biruni Sol.475.(c) Ibn Battuta, a traveler from Morocco, authored a book of travels called Rihla, which is written in Arabic and provides rich and fascinating details about the social and cultural life in the subcontinent during the 14th century. Muhammad Bin Tughlaq was the ruler of the Delhi sultanate when Ibn Batuta came to India. The Sultan was impressed by his scholarship, and appointed him the qazi or judge of Delhi.

Prithviraj Raso. It is a Braj language epic poem about the life of Prithviraj Chauhan. Other Ancient Books and Authors : ‘Ain-i-Akbari’ - Abul Fazl, ‘Mahabharata’ - Vyasa, ‘Ramayana’ - Valmiki, ‘Shakuntala’ - Kalidasa, and ‘Mudrarakshas’ - Vishakhadatta.

Sawai Jaisingh II founded the city of Jaipur on 18 November 1727 AD. He was appointed the Subedar (governor) of Agra in 1722. He established five Jantar Mantar observatories, located in Delhi, Jaipur, Ujjain, Mathura, and Varanasi.

Chahamana. Bijoliya Inscription reveals that Chauhans are Vats Gotriya Brahman. This inscription provides information about the names of Jabalipur, Shakmbhari, Srimal. An inscription of Vikram Samvat Falgun Krishna Tritiya is inscribed on a rock near the northern wall of the ancient Parshvanath temple located in Bijolia.

Alauddin Hasan Bahman Shah founded the Bahmani Sultanate in 1347. Its capital was Ahsanabad (Gulbarga) from 1347 to 1425, later shifted to Muhammadabad (Bidar). Q.480. Which State was founded by Nizam-ul-Mulk in 1724? SSC CPO 27/06/2024 (3rd shift) (a) Mysore (b) Hyderabad (c) Bengal (d) Awadh Sol.480.(b) Hyderabad. Nizam-ul-Mulk Asaf Jah (Chin Qilich Khan) was one of the most powerful members at the court of the Mughal Emperor Farrukh Siyar. He was entrusted first with the governorship of Awadh, and later given charge of the Deccan.

Tarafdars. The Bahmani Kingdom, established in by Ala-ud-din Hassan Bahman Shah after revolting against the Delhi Sultanate. It was divided into four administrative regions known as "taraf." These regions included Daulatabad, Bidar, Berar, and Gulbarga, each governed by a tarafdar or subedar. Q.482. Who among the following is known as Tuti-e-Hind ? SSC CPO 29/06/2024 (1st shift) (a) Surdas (b) Tansen (c) Amir Khusro (d) Naubat Khan Sol.482.(c) Amir Khusro is one of India's greatest Persian-language poets. His books - Tughlaq Nama, Khazain-ul-Fatuh. Other writers: Surdas - Sur Sagar, Sur-Saravali. Tansen - Tashrih-ul-moosiqui.

Junagarh Fort. It is located in Bikaner, Rajasthan. It was originally called Chintamani and later renamed to mean "Old Fort." Amer Fort: It is situated History in Amer near Jaipur, was initially built by Raja Man Singh and expanded by Sawai Jai Singh. Kumbhalgarh Fort: It is located in Rajsamand district, was constructed by Rana Kumbha in the 15th century, with chief architect Mandan showcasing his style in Raja Vallabh.

5. After the death of Muhammad Shah-III (ruler) in 1482, the Kingdom was disintegrated into five kingdoms: Bijapur, Ahmednagar, Bera, Golconda and Bidar. Q.485. SSC CPO 04/10/2023 (Morning) (a) Ahmad Shah (b) Humayun (c) Mujahid Shah (d) Firuz Shah Sol.485.(a) Ahmad Shah (1422-1435). Bahmani sultanate was established by Alauddin Hasan Bahman Shah (Zafar Khan) in 1347. Bahmani kingdom comprises the parts of the current day’s Karnataka, Maharashtra, and Andhra Pradesh.

Portuguese - They were the first Europeans to come to India by Sea routes in 1498, led by Vasco da Gama at Calicut (Kerala). European nations arrived in India after Portuguese: Dutch, English, Danish, and French. The Portuguese were also the last to leave India in 1961.

Siraj-ud-Daulah (Reign: 1756 -1757) - Last independent Nawab of Bengal. Alivardi Khan - Nawab of Bengal from 1740 to 1756. He is known for his victory during the battle of Burdwan against Maratha Empire during the Maratha invasion of Bengal. Mir Qasim - Nawab of Bengal (1760-1763).

Char Minar–1664 CE. It was built by Sultan Mohammed Quli Qutb Shah in 1591. It was built in honour of his wife, Bhagmati in Hyderabad. Shaheed Minar: It was built in the memory of Major-general Sir David Ochterlony, commander of the British East India Company.
Revenue Assignment. Other Type of Tax : Kara - It was a generic term used for taxes. Hiranya - Tax on special produce taken in cash. Chat - Security Tax. Prataya -Toll Tax.

French (Last European to arrive in India). Governor-General Dupleix was appointed in 1742. French East India Company : Established in - 1664. The First French Factory - Surat (1668), Second Factory - Masulipatam (1669), Headquarters - Pondicherry. Europeans in India - Portuguese (1498), Dutch (1602), British (1608), Danes (1616).

Jat revolt (Mathura). In 1669 CE against Aurangzeb. Satnami revolt (1672) - The Satnamis were a sect comprising peasants, artisans and untouchables who rebelled against the Aurangzeb in Narnaul (Haryana). Bundela Rebellion (1842) - Jawahar Singh Bundela of Chandrapur and Madhukar Shah of Narhat were the leaders of the revolt.

Ferdowsi (Court poet of Mahmud of Ghazni) - A Persian poet and the author of Shahnameh ("Book of Kings''), which is one of the world's longest epic poems created by a single poet, and the greatest epic of Persian-speaking countries. Q.493. Malik Ambar, who resisted Mughals in Deccan, was an able administrator of which state? SSC CGL 18/07/2023 (1st shift) (a) Bidar (b) Bijapur (c) Golconda (d) Ahmednagar Sol.493.(d) Ahmednagar. Malik Ambar was an African-origin statesman and military leader who served as the regent of the Nizam Shahi dynasty in the Deccan, of Ahmednagar (now part of Maharashtra).

Berar (Maharashtra). Imad Shahi Dynasty: Established by - Fathullah Imad Ul Mulk (1490). He fortified Gavilgad and Narnala. Berar Sultanate was annexed by Sultan Murtaza Shah of Ahmednagar Kingdom in 1574. Capital - Ellichpur.

Raag Tarangini - Initiated an alternative system of the division of rags, 'the Thaat system'. Lochan Kavi (Lochan Panḍit) belonged to the Mithila region (Bihar). “Sangeet Darpan” was written by Damodar Pandit. “Anup Rag Vilas” is written by Kumar Gandharva.
Murshid Quli Khan (Zamin Ali Quli) was born as Surya Narayan Mishra; the first Nawab of Bengal (1717 to 1727). He built the Katra Masjid (1723-1724) where he was buried after his death.

Aurobindo Ghose was an Indian philosopher, yogi, poet, and nationalist. He also worked as a journalist, editing newspapers such as Bande Mataram. The Alipore Bomb Case, also known as the Muraripukur Conspiracy or Manicktolla Bomb Conspiracy, was a significant criminal trial in India in 1908, involving several Indian nationalists. Q.848. When was the All Bengal Students Conference held in the chairmanship of Jawaharlal Nehru? SSC MTS 01/10/2024 (2nd Shift) (a) 1928 (b) 1939 (c) 1934 (d) 1920 Sol.848.(a) 1928. All Bengal Students Conference - It was organized in response to the expulsion of students from Kolkata colleges for "unruly conduct" and the subsequent student protests. The conference led to the formation of the All-Bengal Students Association to unite and organize students across the region. Q.849. In 1927, where was the conference against colonial oppression and imperialism, in which Jawaharlal History Nehru participated on behalf of Indian National Congress, held? SSC MTS 29/10/2024 (1st Shift) (a) Bruges (b) Leuven (c) Antwerp (d) Brussels Sol.849.(d) Brussels. Pt. Jawaharlal Nehru became General Secretary of the All India Congress Committee in 1923. In 1926, he toured several countries, including Belgium, where he attended the Congress of Oppressed Nationalities in 1927. He also participated in the 10th anniversary of the October Revolution in Moscow. At the 1927 Madras Congress, Nehru advocated for full independence.

1917. Women’s Indian Association, a heritage welfare organization of the women by the women for the women. It was formed on 8th May 1917 at Adyar, Chennai by Annie Besent. It was the first organization to create an overall awakening among women and to train them to shoulder their responsibility in public services.

Rajendra Prasad. The Akhil Bharatiya Kalidas Samaroh is an annual cultural festival held in Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh, since 1958, celebrating the works of poet Kalidasa. Q.852. Who became the Nawab of Bengal after the death of Alivardi Khan in the year 1756? SSC MTS 12/11/2024 (1st Shift) (a) Shuja-Ud-Daulah (b) Siraj-Ud-Daulah (c) Saadat Ali Khan (d) Wazir Ali Sol.852.(b) Siraj-Ud-Daulah. Following Alivardi Khan's death in 1756, Sirajuddaulah became Nawab of Bengal but faced conflict with the British, who aimed to install a puppet ruler.

15th August. India gained independence from British rule on August 15, 1947, ending nearly two centuries of colonial control. The transfer of power also led to the partition, creating India and Pakistan.

Dinabandhu Mitra. Nil Darpan is a Bengali-language play written in 1858-59 by Dinabandhu Mitra, depicting the oppression of indigo farmers by British planters in Bengal. His other works include ‘Nabin Tapasvini’, ‘Biye Pagla Budo’, ‘Sadhabar Ekadashi’, and ‘Kamale Kamini’. Other authors and their works : Sarojini Naidu - ‘The Golden Threshold’, ‘The Bird of Time’. Chittaranjan Das - ‘India for Indians’, ‘Freedom Through Disobedience’. Q.855. Who among the following freedom fighters of India is the author of the book, 'The Indian Struggle' ? SSC CGL 10/09/2024 (2nd Shift) (a) Mahatma Gandhi (b) Motilal Nehru (c) Vallabhbhai Patel (d) Subhas Chandra Bose Sol.855.(d) Subhas Chandra Bose. His Books : An Indian Pilgrim, Jaruri Kichu Lekha, Famous Speeches and Letters of Subhas Chandra Bose. Q.856. Which of the following books is INCORRECTLY paired with its respective author? SSC CGL 10/09/2024 (3rd Shift) (a) Hind Swaraj – Mahatma Gandhi (b) Gitanjali – Rabindranath Tagore (c) The Discovery of India – Subhash Chandra Bose (d) Why I am an Atheist – Bhagat Singh Sol.856.(c) ‘’The Discovery of India’’ is a book by Jawaharlal Nehru.
A-iv, B-iii, C-ii, D-i.

Odisha. The Khurda Revolt of 1817 was an armed uprising against the British, sparked by the rapid and disruptive changes introduced by the East India Company, which agitated the local population. This revolt also impacted other districts of Odisha, such as Cuttack. It is also known as the Paika Rebellion or Paika Bidroha, and it was led by Bakshi Jagabandhu.

Benaras. The Hindu College was founded by Jonathan Duncan, a British officer, and Raja Rameshwar Singh, the Maharaja of Banaras. The college was originally known as the Sanskrit College, and it was dedicated to the study of Sanskrit language and literature. In 1958, the Sanskrit College became a university and was renamed Sampurnanand Sanskrit University in 1974. Raja Ram Mohan Roy established the Hindu College in Calcutta in 1817, which is now known as Presidency University, Kolkata.

BR Ambedkar was an Indian jurist and social reformer who served as the first Minister of Law and Justice and was the chief architect of the Constitution of India. He was awarded the Bharat Ratna posthumously in 1990. Organizations and Founders: Swatantra Shramik Party (B.R. Ambedkar), Swarajya Party (Motilal Nehru and Chittaranjan Das), Muslim League (Agha Khan & Salimullah), Indian National Congress (A.O.Hume).

1920. All India Trade Union Congress (AITUC) was formed in Bombay. The AITUC was a broad-based organisation involving diverse ideologies. The main ideological groups were the communists led by S.A. Dange and M.N. Roy, the moderates led by M. Joshi and V.V. Giri and the nationalists which involved people like Lala Lajpat Rai and Jawaharlal Nehru.

Madame HP Blavatsky and Colonel Olcott. The Theosophical Society, emphasized the study of ancient Hindu, Buddhist, and Zoroastrian philosophies. It advocated for universal brotherhood based on principles from the Upanishads and Vedas. Annie Besant joined the society in 1889 and first came to India on 16 November 1893. Its headquarters in India was established in Adyar, Madras (now Chennai). Ramabai Ranade and G. K. Devadhar were both founders of the Poona Seva Sadan.

Swadeshi movement. The Swadeshi movement, led by Gandhiji, aimed to promote self-sufficiency by boycotting British goods and encouraging Indian-made products. India's first cotton mill, the Bombay Spinning and Weaving Company, was established in Mumbai on July 7, 1854. The world’s first cotton mill, the Upper Priory Cotton Mill, was founded by Lewis Paul and John Wyatt in Birmingham, England, in 1741. Q.864. ________ is one of the first criticism of the caste system written by Mahatma Phule and published in the year 1873. SSC CHSL 01/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) Jati ka Unmoolan (b) Bharat Mein Jaati Evam Prajaati (c) Gulami Ki Kahani (d) Gulamgiri Sol.864.(d) Gulamgiri (Marathi Language), also known as Slavery, summarizes the sufferings of lower-caste people and is written in Marathi. Phule formed the Satyashodhak Samaj in 1873. He, together with his wife Savitribai Phule, opened a girls' school in Poona.
Dadabhai Naoroji - He was known as the "Grand Old Man of India”. He introduced thе 'drain thеory' in his rеnownеd book "Povеrty and thе Un-British Rulе in India”.

Rabindranath Tagore. He wrote the Indian national anthem, "Jana Gana Mana" in Bengali in 1911, and it was officially adopted as the national anthem of India on January 24, 1950. Q.867. Which Commission drew the boundary line between India and Pakistan? SSC CHSL 11/07/2024 (1st shift) (a) Radcliffe Boundary Commission (b) Gandhi Irwin Pact (c) Hilton Young Commission (d) Bretton woods Commission Sol.867.(a) Radcliffe Boundary Commission. The "Radcliffe Line" became the border between India and Pakistan the partition of India on 17 August 1947. The Border Commission headed by Sir Cyril Radcliffe. Gandhi-Irwin Pact (1931 - 32) was an agreement between Mahatma Gandhi and Lord Irwin. The Hilton Young Commission (1926) recommended the establishment of the 'Reserve Bank of India'. The Bretton Woods Committee (BWC in 1944) created two organizations - the International Monetary Fund (IMF) and the World Bank.

Reginald Dyer. The Jallianwala Bagh massacre (Amritsar massacre) took place on April 13, 1919 (spring festival of Baisakhi) in Punjab. It happened under the rule of Lt. Governor of Punjab, Michael O’Dwyer, and viceroy of India, Lord Chelmsford. People gathered there to protest against the Rowlatt Act and arrests of pro-Indian independence leaders Dr. Saifuddin Kitchlew and Dr. Satya Pal.

Portugal. Vasco da Gama arrived in India at Calicut (Kozhikode) in 1498 AD during the reign of the Zamorin, a Hindu ruler. This event marked the discovery of the sea route from Europe to India. He reached India by sea via the "Cape of Good Hope". Francisco de Almeida was appointed as the first Portuguese governor in India. His policy is known for implementing the "Policy of Blue Water," which aimed to establish Portuguese dominance in maritime trade. Q.870. Tattvabodhini Patrika promoted the study of India’s past in which language? SSC CPO 28/06/2024 (3rd shift) (a) Sanskrit (b) Hindi (c) English (d) Bengali Sol.870.(d) Bengali. Tattvabodhini Patrika was a Bengali monthly that started publishing from Calcutta (now Kolkata) in 1843 as a mouthpiece of Debendranath Tagore's Tattvabodhini Sabha.

Annie Besant first came to India on 16 November 1893. She joined the Theosophical Society in May 1889 History and founded a weekly newspaper, Commonweal, in January 1914 for her political work. Lala Lajpat Rai - The shots that hit me are the last nails to the coffin of British rule in India.
Jonathan Duncan. The Sanskrit College at Varanasi was established in 1791. The Hindu College (now Presidency University) in Kolkata was founded in 1817.

Edwin Lutyens (an English architect). Other buildings designed by him: Rashtrapati Bhavan, India Gate, The Cenotaph Whitehall (London). The Old Parliament of India was also designed by Herbert Baker and Lutyens. The foundation stone of India Gate was laid by His Royal Highness, the Duke of Connaught in 1921.

Subhash Chandra Bose. All India Forward Bloc was a Political Party formed on May 3, 1939 in Makur Unnao, Uttar Pradesh. Its formation was announced in Calcutta.

Satyendra Nath Tagore Qualified in 1863. Indian Services Act, reserved certain positions for covenanted civil servants. It was enacted in during Lord Canning's Viceroyalty. Mahatma Gandhi. Muhammad Iqbal - He wrote the famous song 'Saare jahan se achha', also known for giving birth to the 'idea of Pakistan'. Muhammad Ali Jinnah was a barrister, politician and the founder of Pakistan. Matric Level 27/06/2023 (Shift - 2) (a) Munda uprising (b) Moplah uprising (c) Santhal uprising (d) Kol uprising Sol.881.(b) Moplah uprising - Led by Variyamkunnath Kunjahammed Haji, against the British and the Hindu landlords; Wagon tragedy - Moplah prisoners died of suffocation during transportation in a closed freight wagon.

Gopal Hari Deshmukh. Balshastri Jambhekar (Father of Marathi Journalism) - He started the very first newspaper, ‘Darpan’, and revolutionized the journalism system in Maharashtra. Q.883. He was the Governor of Bombay.

Narayan Malhar Joshi - He was an Indian trade union leader, Founded social service league (1911). He started the All India Trade Union Congress (1920). He was the general secretary of AITUC. Manabendra Nath Roy - leader of India’s communists until the independence of India in 1947.

12 percent. The population census, a Union subject under Article 246 of the Indian Constitution, conducted its first census of Independent India in 1951, listed as serial number 69 in the seventh schedule. This census revealed the population of the country to be 36, 10, 88, 090.
Nightingale of India (Bharat Kokila). This title was given to Sarojini Naidu by Mahatma Gandhi. Notable Works - The Golden Threshold, The Broken Wing.

Maharashtra. Deccan Riots: Began at - Supa village (Poona) in 1875. The farmers’ main motive was to destroy the account books of the moneylenders and they also socially boycotted the moneylenders. The movement was supported by the Poona Sarvajanik Sabha (co-founded by M G Ranade).
Bal Gangadhar Tilak.

Famine. 1770 Bengal Famine - It affected Bengal and Bihar. Relief - Attempts to prevent export and hoard or monopolize grain; 15,000 was spent on the import of food grains. Q.890. Mahar. It is listed as the Scheduled Caste in India. Dr. B.R Ambedkar - Architect of Indian Constitution.
Syed Ahmed Khan was the founder of Aligarh Muslim University and a forward looking Islamist reformer. Mohammed Ali Jinnah - First governor - general (1947–48) of Pakistan.
Mother Teresa (from Macedonia) - Nobel Peace Prize (1979), founded the Missionaries of Charity. Saint Bernadette - French saint whose visions led to the founding of the Marian shrine of Lourdes.

1859 (March). The Indigo Rebellion (Neel Bidroh) started in Bengal and was a revolt by the farmers against British planters who had forced them to grow indigo under terms that were greatly unfavorable to the farmers.

at Paris under the patronage of Madam Bhikaji Cama, Munchershah Burjorji Godrej and S. R. Rana. It was a branch of the Indian Home Rule Society which was established in London in 1905. Bhikaji Cama's important publication is 'Vande Mataram' (1909).

1905. Other revolutionaries: V.D. Savarkar, Bhikaji Cama, V.N. Chatterjee, Lala Har Dayal, V.V.S. Aiyar, M.P.T. Acharya, P.M. Bapat, etc. Shyamji Krishna Verma founded the Indian Home Rule Society and The Indian Sociologist (Journal) in London. He became the first President of Bombay Arya Samaj.

Rabindranath Tagore. Rabindranath Tagore: Santiniketan (1901), Nobel Prize in Literature (1913, Gitanjali), composed Jana Gana Mana (India’s National Anthem) and Amar Shonar Bangla (Bangladesh’s National Anthem). Other Works: Manasi (1890) [The Ideal One], Sonar Tari (1894) [The Golden Boat], Gitimalya (1914) [Wreath of Songs], and Balaka (1916) [The Flight of Cranes]. Q.898. The claim for ‘swarajya’ or self-government within the British Empire, on the model of self-governing colonies like Australia and Canada, was put forward from the Congress platform in 1905 by: SSC CPO 10/11/2022 (Afternoon) (a) Lala Lajpat Rai (b) Dadabhai Naoroji (c) Bipin Chandra Pal (d) Gopal Krishna Gokhale Sol.898.(d) Gopal Krishna Gokhale (Diamond of India) was Political Guru of Gandhi, Congress President (1905), established Servants of India Society in 1905, English Newspaper (The Hitavada), founded The Ranade Institute of Economics (1905), ‘Dharmatma Gokhale’ (Writer - Mahatma Gandhi).

Acharya Vinoba Bhave in 1951, the Bhoodan Movement (first started in Andhra Pradesh) was initiated at Pochampally village (Telangana). The Bhoodan movement attempted to persuade wealthy landowners to voluntarily give a percentage of their land to landless people.

Lala Lajpat Rai. All India Trade Union Congress, AITUC (created on October 31, 1920 in Bombay) is the first Central Trade Union of India and the second largest trade union federation in India after the Indian National Trade union Congress. In India, Trade Unions in India are registered under the Trade Union Act (1926).

Dadabhai Naoroji. He was known as India Gladstone. The first attempt to calculate National Income of India was made by him. He was the first Asian (Indian) member of the British Parliament. He coined the name “Indian National Congress” (INC).
Matangini Hazra. Matangini Hazra was an Indian revolutionary and social activist who participated in the Indian independence movement.

1920. Oudh Kisan Sabha (Oudh Farmers Association) was an association formed by Baba Ram Chandra. He was a trade unionist who organized the farmers of Oudh and led the first protest against the landlords.

Shimla Pact was signed in 1972 by Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto, the President of Pakistan, and Indira Gandhi, the Prime Minister of India. Q.905. What was the name of the operation that led to the annexation of the Hyderabad State into India? It was a military operation in which the Indian Armed Forces invaded the Nizam-ruled princely state, annexing it into the Indian Union.
Peasants’ uprising. It was against the tax policies of the British in 1894. Patharughat is a place in Assam and is currently known as Patharighat.
Nyaya Yudh. He founded the Lok Dal party.
French. Battle of Chandernagore (1757) - Between French and British (led by Robert Clive) during the Seven Years' War (1756-1763).
1944. The outcome of the battle of Imphal was a turning point of one of the most gruelling campaigns of the Second World War (1939-45).
1960. After this act, Bombay State was dissolved and split on linguistic lines into the two states of Gujarat (Gujarati speaking population) and Maharashtra (Marathi speaking population).

Shyama Prasad Mukherjee. BJS was an Indian right-wing political party that existed from 1951 to 1977 and was the political arm of Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS), a Hindu nationalist volunteer organization.

Santhals (Tribal who inhabited the forest of Rajmahal hills). Damin-i-Koh - British promised non-interference in the land. Q.913. Fort Saint Louis was a ______ fort that stood in Pondicherry on the eastern coast of India. SSC CHSL 16/04/2021 (Morning) (a) French (b) Danish (c) Dutch (d) British Sol.913.(a) French. Fort Saint Louis - Built by Francois Martin History and Destroyed by the Britishers in 1761.

Peasants. Ryots were Individual cultivators who had full rights regarding sale, transfer, and leasing of the land as long as they paid the rent under the Ryotwari system (Land revenue paid by the farmers directly to the state).
Shah Jahan Begum. Nawab of Bhopal that ruled – Nawab Sikandar Begum:- 1860-1868, Begum Sultan Shah Jehan:-1844-1860 and 1868-1901. Begum Kaikhusrau Jahan:- 1901-1926, Begum Sajida Sultan:- 1961-1995.

Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishan (1st Vice President of India) was one of the three main speakers at the Central Hall of Parliament - Other than JL Nehru (Tryst with Destiny Speech) and Chaudhary Khaliquzaman.
Jawaharlal Nehru. The aim of this committee was to make a plan which could balance private and public partnership and urban and rural economies.

Gwalior. The Samadhi of Rani Lakshmi Bai is situated at Phool Bagh. It is to commemorate the great warrior of Jhansi. Q.919. The princely state of Hyderabad was brought under the Indian Union in the year ______. SSC CPO 24/11/2020 (Morning) (a) 1950 (b) 1952 (c) 1948 (d) 1963 Sol.919.(c) 1948. After the State Reorganisation Act 1956 Hyderabad was merged with Andhra state and later renamed as Andhra Pradesh.

Rajagopalachari's formula (or C. R. formula or Rajaji formula) was a proposal formulated by Chakravarti Rajagopalachari to solve the political deadlock between the All India Muslim League and the Indian National Congress on the independence of British India.
Rabindranath Tagore became the first non-European to receive the Nobel Prize for his literary work. He was awarded knighthood in 1915 by King George V.

Maharashtra. Savithribai Phule - She was the first female teacher of India and opened a school for women in 1848, Married to Jyotirao Phule (Founded Satya Shodhak Samaj in 1873 in Pune). Q.923. Who among the following scholars is associated with the Tibet-home theory of the Aryans? SSC CGL 07/03/2020 (Evening) (a) Dayanand Saraswati (b) Bal Gangadhar Tilak (c) MacDonell (d) Max Muller Sol.923.(a) Swami Dayanand Saraswati in his book Satyarth Prakash propounded that the original abode of the Aryans was Tibet. The reasoning behind this was that due to the extreme cold in Tibet, sun and fire were worshiped and the flora and fauna described in the Rigveda were found in Tibet.

Lord Wavell. The members of the Cabinet Mission were Lord Penthick Lawrence, Secretary of State for India, Sir Stafford Cripps, President of the Board of Trade, and A.V Alexander, First Lord of Admiralty.
